gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (416/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (416/ 9)
text: مَنْ هَمَّ بِسَيِّئَةٍ فَلَا يَعْمَلْهَا فَإِنَّهُ رُبَّمَا عَمِلَ الْعَبْدُ السَّيِّئَةَ فَيَرَاهُ الرَّبُّ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فَيَقُولُ وَ عِزَّتِي وَ جَلَالِي لَا أَغْفِرُ لَكَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ أَبَداً .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who thinks of an evil deed, so he should not do it, for perhaps the servant does the evil deed, and the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High Sees him and He<sup>azwj</sup> would be Saying: “By My<sup>azwj</sup> Honour and My<sup>azwj</sup> Majesty, I<sup>azwj</sup> will not Forgive you after that, ever!”’.
en.sarwar: 17. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “One who thinks to commit a sin, must not commit it; perhaps a servant (of Allah) commits a sin and the Lord, the Most Blessed, the Most High, finds him in that condition. He will say, ‘I swear by my Majesty and Glory that I will never forgive you thereafter.’”
